quiz.title: Unit 3 Quiz 5, Biology I
quiz.subtitle: Biochemistry: Macromolecules & Polymers
quiz.subject: Biology
quiz.topic: Carbohydrates, Lipids, Proteins, Nucleic Acids, Dehydration Synthesis, Hydrolysis

questions.question: Which reaction forms polymers by removing water molecules?
questions.explanation: Dehydration synthesis (also called condensation) joins monomers by removing a water molecule — one monomer loses an -OH, the other loses an -H, forming a covalent bond.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a function of lipids in the body?
options: form part of the cell membrane
questions.explanation: Speeding up chemical reactions is the function of enzymes (proteins), not lipids. Lipids do insulate, store energy long-term, and form cell membranes (phospholipids).

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a type of lipid?
questions.explanation: Glycogen is a polysaccharide (carbohydrate), not a lipid. Steroids (like cholesterol), triglycerides (fats and oils), and phospholipids (membrane components) are all major classes of lipids.

questions.question: Which reaction breaks down polymers into monomers by adding water?
questions.explanation: Hydrolysis ('water splitting') breaks polymers apart by adding water molecules — the -OH attaches to one monomer, the -H to the other, breaking the covalent bond.

questions.question: Carbohydrates are composed of which ratio of elements?
questions.explanation: Carbohydrates have the general formula (CH₂O)n, meaning for every carbon atom, there are approximately two hydrogen atoms and one oxygen atom — a 1:2:1 ratio.

questions.question: Which glycosidic bond orientation in glucose polymers is digestible by humans?
questions.explanation: Humans produce enzymes (amylase) that break alpha-glycosidic bonds found in starch and glycogen. Beta-glycosidic bonds in cellulose cannot be digested by humans.

questions.question: Which polysaccharide provides structural support in plants?
questions.explanation: Cellulose forms strong fibers in plant cell walls due to beta-glycosidic bonds and hydrogen bonding between parallel chains. Starch is energy storage, glycogen is animal energy storage, maltose is a disaccharide.

questions.question: Triglycerides are primarily used for:
questions.explanation: Triglycerides (fats and oils) store energy long-term because they pack more energy per gram than carbohydrates and are hydrophobic, so they don't add water weight.

questions.question: Proteins are polymers of:
questions.explanation: Proteins are made of amino acid monomers linked by peptide bonds. Nucleotides form nucleic acids, fatty acids form lipids, monosaccharides form carbohydrates.

questions.question: Which level of protein structure is determined by interactions between amino acid side chains?
questions.explanation: Tertiary structure results from interactions between R-groups (side chains) — including hydrogen bonds, ionic bonds, hydrophobic interactions, and disulfide bridges. Primary is sequence, secondary is alpha helices/beta sheets, quaternary is multiple polypeptide interactions.

questions.question: DNA stores genetic information in the form of:
questions.explanation: The sequence of nitrogenous bases (A, T, G, C) along DNA encodes genetic information. The sugar-phosphate backbone is structural, not informational.

questions.question: RNA differs from DNA because it:
questions.explanation: RNA can fold into complex 3D structures (like tRNA and rRNA) that perform catalytic and structural functions. DNA typically remains double-stranded. RNA uses uracil, ribose sugar, and is usually single-stranded.

questions.question: A molecule with long hydrocarbon chains, insoluble in water, and high energy content is likely a:
questions.explanation: Lipids (fats, oils, phospholipids) are characterized by long hydrocarbon chains, hydrophobic behavior, and high energy storage capacity. Carbohydrates and proteins are hydrophilic; nucleic acids are charged.

questions.question: What is the monomer of carbohydrates?
questions.explanation: Monosaccharides (simple sugars like glucose) are carbohydrate monomers. They link via glycosidic bonds to form disaccharides and polysaccharides.

questions.question: What bond links amino acids in proteins?
questions.explanation: Peptide bonds form between the carboxyl group of one amino acid and the amino group of another via dehydration synthesis, creating polypeptide chains.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You understand the structure and function of all four macromolecule types.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Review dehydration synthesis vs. hydrolysis and polymer-monomer relationships.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on the monomers and bonds specific to each macromolecule.
scoreMessages.0: Please review the four major classes of biological macromolecules and their building blocks.
